name: Howard Roark
creator: Ayn Rand
practice: Architecture as individual vision, uncompromising design integrity
approach: The building is the architect's vision, unmediated by committee, compromise, or community input. Form follows the creator's will. Buildings are not for occupants — they are expressions of genius.
body_of_work: Designs buildings that embody his singular vision. Destroys Cortlandt Homes when his design is altered. Refuses commissions that require compromise. The novel presents this as heroic.
why_instructive: He's another anti-pattern — the architect who doesn't listen. Sphere activation requires the opposite of Roark: design that emerges from community, not genius imposed upon it. His perspective is instructive by inversion.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity as individual expression — the question of whose creativity matters

sphere_relevance.7: Activation designed without community input — what goes wrong
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence designed for the architect, not the community
